openmeter/registry.purpose: Organisational folder owning the entitlement registry factory — a non-Wire DI entry point that wires all entitlement-domain adapters, connectors, and services into a single *registry.Entitlement struct for use by the balance-worker and test suites that need entitlement infrastructure without importing app/common.

patterns.name: Single-function factory package
patterns.description: One exported function GetEntitlementRegistry in builder/ produces a fully-populated *registry.Entitlement; callers never construct the struct manually.

patterns.name: Options struct as constructor input
patterns.description: All dependencies (DatabaseClient, StreamingConnector, etc.) are collected in a flat EntitlementOptions struct, not variadic functional options.

patterns.name: Bottom-up adapter initialisation order
patterns.description: Adapters are constructed before connectors, connectors before services; no circular dependency at runtime.

patterns.name: Register hooks after connector construction
patterns.description: entitlementsubscriptionhook and credithook must be registered after the connector is built but before the struct is returned.

patterns.name: transactionManager via enttx.NewCreator
patterns.description: Always wrap opts.DatabaseClient with enttx.NewCreator — never pass the raw *entdb.Client as the TxCreator.

key_file_guides.role: The sole factory function GetEntitlementRegistry that wires every entitlement sub-service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing hook registrations after connector construction; partial population of the returned struct causing nil-dereferences downstream.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common from this package — it must remain independent to avoid import cycles with test suites
Skipping hook registration after connector construction — credithook and entitlementsubscriptionhook are mandatory for correct credit burn-down and subscription sync
Passing opts.DatabaseClient directly as a TxCreator — always wrap with enttx.NewCreator
Adding business logic or conditional wiring inside GetEntitlementRegistry — conditionals belong in the caller
Returning a partially populated *registry.Entitlement — every field must be set or downstream nil-dereferences will occur

decisions.decision: Single-function package instead of Wire provider set
decisions.rationale: balance-worker and test suites need entitlement infrastructure without the full app/common import graph; a standalone factory avoids import cycles while keeping wiring centralised.

decisions.decision: EntitlementOptions flat struct over variadic functional options
decisions.rationale: Flat struct makes all required dependencies explicit and compile-time checkable without the indirection of functional option closures.

openmeter/secret.purpose: Manages encrypted secrets (e.g., Stripe API keys) for installed marketplace apps. The root package owns the Service and Adapter interfaces; sub-packages provide the stub plaintext adapter, pure domain types (entity/), and the thin validation-only service implementation (service/).

patterns.name: Interface segregation: Adapter embeds SecretAdapter
patterns.description: secret.Adapter embeds secret.SecretAdapter; new storage operations must be added to SecretAdapter, not directly to Adapter.

patterns.name: Validate-then-delegate in every method
patterns.description: service/ calls input.Validate() unconditionally before delegating to the adapter — the adapter is a stub and won't catch bad inputs.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return secretentity.SecretID{}, err }; return s.adapter.CreateAppSecret(ctx, input)

patterns.name: Config struct constructor with Validate()
patterns.description: service.New accepts a Config struct and calls Validate() immediately — the only nil-safety gate before storing the adapter.
patterns.example: func New(cfg Config) (Service, error) { if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &service{adapter: cfg.Adapter}, nil }

patterns.name: Pure domain types in entity/ sub-package
patterns.description: All input structs, SecretID, Secret, and SecretNotFoundError live in openmeter/secret/entity — no persistence logic or Ent imports.

patterns.name: SecretID encodes plaintext value (stub adapter)
patterns.description: The plaintext adapter stores the raw secret value as the ID field of SecretID — a known stub convention until a real secret store is wired.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Adapter and SecretAdapter interfaces consumed by the service layer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding persistence logic here — storage belongs in adapter sub-packages, not the interface file.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Service and SecretService interfaces.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New operations must be added to SecretService first, then implemented in service/.

key_file_guides.role: Pure domain types: SecretID, Secret, TaxCodeAppMapping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Importing openmeter/ent/db or any persistence package — this file must stay dependency-free.

key_file_guides.role: All input structs (CreateAppSecretInput, UpdateAppSecretInput, etc.) each with a Validate() method.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New input structs added without a Validate() method — service layer calls Validate() unconditionally.

key_file_guides.role: SecretNotFoundError wrapping models.GenericNotFoundError for HTTP status mapping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returning raw errors instead of models.NewGenericNotFoundError — breaks HTTP status code mapping.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic or cross-domain calls to service/ — it is a thin validation+delegation layer only
Injecting *entdb.Client directly into Service — persistence is the adapter's concern
Importing openmeter/ent/db in entity/ — it must remain a pure domain type package
Returning raw errors from stub adapter instead of nil — callers expect CreateAppSecret and DeleteAppSecret to succeed
Bypassing the Config struct and constructing service{} directly — Validate() in New() is the only nil-safety gate

decisions.decision: Input types live in a separate entity/ sub-package
decisions.rationale: Keeps the root secret package to pure interfaces while allowing adapter and service sub-packages to import input types without circular dependencies.

decisions.decision: Plaintext stub adapter stores value in SecretID.ID
decisions.rationale: Allows the rest of the app to function without a real secret store during development; a future production adapter can replace this without changing the Adapter interface.

openmeter/server.purpose: Chi-based HTTP server package that assembles the v1 and v3 REST APIs behind a shared middleware stack (auth, OpenAPI validation, CORS, logging). The server/ root owns the Chi router construction, middleware wiring, and dual-version route registration; the router/ sub-package is the pure v1 endpoint delegation layer.

patterns.name: Dual-version route registration in NewServer
patterns.description: NewServer mounts the v3 API via v3server.NewServer + RegisterRoutes first, then wraps the v1 API with api.HandlerWithOptions in a separate Chi Group so each version has its own middleware chain.
patterns.example: v3API.RegisterRoutes(r); api.HandlerWithOptions(impl, api.ChiServerOptions{BaseRouter: r, Middlewares: middlewares})

patterns.name: Config aggregates every service reference
patterns.description: server.Config embeds router.Config which holds every domain service interface; all fields are validated via Config.Validate() before the router is created.

patterns.name: StaticNamespaceDecoder for both versions
patterns.description: namespacedriver.StaticNamespaceDecoder(defaultNS) is injected into both v1 router.Config and v3server.Config — never resolve namespace inside a handler.

patterns.name: RFC 7807 error responses via models.NewStatusProblem
patterns.description: All non-handler errors (404, 405, param decode failures) call models.NewStatusProblem(ctx, err, status).Respond(w); the errorHandlerReply function maps oapi-codegen error types to status codes.

patterns.name: OapiRequestValidatorWithOptions with NoopAuthenticationFunc
patterns.description: OpenAPI schema validation runs after the portal/API-key authenticator middleware; authentication is delegated to NoopAuthenticationFunc so the validator never rejects auth-related requests.

patterns.name: corsHandler with AllowedPaths filter
patterns.description: CORS is gated to specific path prefixes (e.g. /api/v1/portal/meters) using corsOptions.AllowedPaths; requests to other paths skip CORS middleware entirely.

key_file_guides.role: NewServer: constructs the Chi router, wires middleware stacks for v1 and v3, and mounts both API versions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding business logic or domain service calls directly here; middleware order matters — auth must precede OapiRequestValidator.

key_file_guides.role: corsHandler with path-prefix filtering; only routes matching AllowedPaths receive CORS headers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Setting AllowedPaths to nil/empty enables CORS for all paths — intentional for the portal use case but dangerous elsewhere.

key_file_guides.role: Integration smoke test for all v1 and v3 routes using an in-memory test server constructed from mock services.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All new endpoints must have a corresponding test case here to verify routing and basic response shapes.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests for httptransport error encoding (ValidationIssue -> HTTP status mapping).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any change to commonhttp.HandleIssueIfHTTPStatusKnown must be reflected here.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic, DB calls, or domain service calls directly in server.go — all logic belongs in domain httpdriver packages
Registering v3 routes inside the v1 Chi Group — each version must have its own group and middleware chain
Skipping models.NewStatusProblem for error responses — all errors must render as application/problem+json
Adding middleware that runs before the authenticator for auth-sensitive paths without updating the PostAuthMiddlewares extension point
Hand-editing api/api.gen.go or api/v3/api.gen.go to add routes — always regenerate from TypeSpec via make gen-api

decisions.decision: v3 server mounted in a separate Chi Group before the v1 group
decisions.rationale: Each API version needs its own middleware chain (v3 uses oasmiddleware.ValidateRequest; v1 uses kin-openapi OapiRequestValidatorWithOptions); sharing a group would mix validator instances.

decisions.decision: ExcludeReadOnlyValidations: true in OpenAPI filter options
decisions.rationale: Go models translate required+readOnly fields to non-nil zero values; excluding read-only validation prevents false rejections on fields that SHOULD NOT appear in requests per the spec.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new v1 endpoint (after gen-api + generate)

openmeter/streaming.purpose: Defines the streaming.Connector interface (ClickHouse-backed usage query and event ingestion) and all query parameter types, plus the Customer/CustomerUsageAttribution attribution model. Concrete implementations live in clickhouse/, a retry decorator in retry/, and an in-memory test double in testutils/.

patterns.name: Connector extends namespace.Handler
patterns.description: streaming.Connector embeds namespace.Handler so every ClickHouse implementation participates in namespace lifecycle (CreateNamespace/DeleteNamespace).
patterns.example: type Connector interface { namespace.Handler; CountEvents(...); QueryMeter(...); BatchInsert(...) }

patterns.name: Validate() on every param type
patterns.description: All Params structs (QueryParams, ListEventsParams, ListEventsV2Params, ListSubjectsParams, ListGroupByValuesParams) implement Validate() returning models.NewNillableGenericValidationError — callers invoke Validate() before passing params to the connector.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Customer interface + CustomerUsageAttribution value type
patterns.description: Customer is an interface requiring GetUsageAttribution() — any type that carries subject/key attribution can be passed to QueryParams.FilterCustomer. CustomerUsageAttribution is the concrete value type with Validate(), GetValues(), and Equal().

patterns.name: FilterStoredAt uses FilterTimeUnix (Unix-second precision)
patterns.description: QueryParams.FilterStoredAt is *filter.FilterTimeUnix, not *filter.FilterTime, because ClickHouse DateTime columns have second precision — always use FilterTimeUnix for stored_at predicates.

patterns.name: Query-struct with toSQL() in clickhouse/ sub-package
patterns.description: Each query type in clickhouse/ is a struct with a toSQL() method using sqlbuilder; never put raw SQL in Connector method bodies — every new query type must follow this pattern.
patterns.example: q := meterQuery{namespace: ns, meter: m, params: p}; sql, args := q.toSQL()

key_file_guides.role: Primary interface definition plus CountEventsParams, RawEvent, ListSubjectsParams, ListGroupByValuesParams with inline Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding new methods here without a matching implementation in clickhouse/, retry/, and testutils/.

key_file_guides.role: QueryParams struct with customer_id group-by constraints (requires FilterCustomer when groupBy=customer_id; max 1 FilterCustomer without customer_id groupBy).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding new group-by dimensions without updating the corresponding Validate() constraint.

key_file_guides.role: ListEventsParams, ListEventsV2Params (cursor-based v2), EventSortField with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: ListEventsParams uses From (required, not pointer) while ListEventsV2Params uses Cursor — do not conflate the two listing APIs.

key_file_guides.role: Customer interface and CustomerUsageAttribution value type; GetValues() returns subject key + key values used for ClickHouse IN clauses.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CustomerUsageAttribution.Validate() requires at least Key or one SubjectKey — creating one without either will fail at the connector layer.

key_file_guides.role: Concrete ClickHouse implementation; wires the query structs to the ClickHouse driver.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Direct ClickHouse errors must be mapped to domain errors (code 60 → NamespaceNotFoundError/MeterNotFoundError) before returning.

key_file_guides.role: MockStreamingConnector for unit tests; AddSimpleEvent/SetSimpleEvents/AddRow/Reset.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MockStreamingConnector truncates StoredAt to Unix seconds — do not rely on sub-second precision in stored-at filter tests.

anti_patterns: Adding raw fmt.Sprintf string interpolation in clickhouse/ query methods — use sqlbuilder.Var() or builder parameters to prevent injection
Wrapping BatchInsert with retry in the retry/ decorator — duplicate event inserts on retry break idempotency
Adding application-level errors (MeterNotFoundError) to the retry predicate — only transient infrastructure errors should be retried
Using filter.FilterTime instead of filter.FilterTimeUnix for stored_at predicates — ClickHouse DateTime has second precision
Returning raw ClickHouse errors to callers — map code 60 to domain error types before returning

decisions.decision: Single shared events table across all namespaces with namespace as leading ORDER BY column
decisions.rationale: Simplifies ClickHouse schema management; namespace as the leading sort key provides efficient per-namespace partition pruning without per-namespace table DDL.

decisions.decision: Query-struct pattern with toSQL() instead of inline SQL in Connector methods
decisions.rationale: Makes each query type independently testable via SQL string assertions in _test.go; prevents inline string interpolation bugs.

decisions.decision: store_row_id (ULID) as cursor tie-breaker in v2 pagination
decisions.rationale: Timestamps alone are not unique at millisecond granularity; ULID provides a monotonic, sortable secondary key without adding a separate auto-increment column.

code_examples.scenario: Building and validating QueryParams with customer filter

openmeter/taxcode.purpose: Manages tax codes (with per-app-type mappings) used during invoice line processing. The root package owns the Service and Repository interfaces, all input/output types, and domain errors as ValidationIssue sentinels; adapter/ provides the Ent/PostgreSQL persistence; service/ provides the business logic layer including system-managed protection and get-or-create with conflict retry.

patterns.name: ValidationIssue sentinels in errors.go
patterns.description: Every domain error is a package-level models.ValidationIssue variable with an explicit ErrorCode, HTTP status attribute, and field path. Constructor functions (NewTaxCodeNotFoundError, NewTaxCodeByAppMappingNotFoundError) attach context attributes.

patterns.name: Validate() on every input type — compile-time assertion
patterns.description: Every input struct implements models.Validator; the root service.go has compile-time var _ models.Validator = (*CreateTaxCodeInput)(nil) assertions for all inputs.

patterns.name: inputOptions embedded in mutable inputs for AllowAnnotations bypass
patterns.description: UpdateTaxCodeInput and DeleteTaxCodeInput embed inputOptions{AllowAnnotations bool} to let internal callers (e.g. system sync) bypass the IsManagedBySystem guard.

patterns.name: transaction.Run at service layer + TransactingRepo in adapter
patterns.description: Service methods wrap adapter calls with transaction.Run for atomicity; each adapter method wraps Ent access with entutils.TransactingRepo so the ctx-bound transaction is honoured.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.repo, func(ctx context.Context) (TaxCode, error) { return s.repo.CreateTaxCode(ctx, input) })

patterns.name: IsManagedBySystem guard in service mutating methods
patterns.description: Before any Update/Delete, service checks tc.IsManagedBySystem() and returns ErrTaxCodeManagedBySystem unless input.AllowAnnotations is true.

patterns.name: JSONB containment query via raw sql.Selector in adapter
patterns.description: GetTaxCodeByAppMapping uses a raw sql.Selector with JSONB @> operator for app-mapping lookups — not a standard Ent predicate.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface, all input types with Validate(), compile-time models.Validator assertions, and inputOptions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New input structs without Validate() or missing compile-time assertions; new mutating methods that skip the IsManagedBySystem guard.

key_file_guides.role: TaxCode domain type, TaxCodeAppMapping/TaxCodeAppMappings with uniqueness validation, IsManagedBySystem, GetAppMapping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TaxCodeStripeRegexp format (txcd_\d{8}) must stay in sync with Stripe's tax code format.

key_file_guides.role: All ValidationIssue sentinels with HTTP status attributes and ErrorCode constants; IsTaxCodeNotFoundError predicate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returning raw errors instead of ValidationIssue sentinels — breaks HTTP status code mapping in the error encoder chain.

key_file_guides.role: Repository interface extending entutils.TxCreator — all adapter implementations must embed a Tx method.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New operations added to Service without a matching Repository method will compile but fail at runtime.

key_file_guides.role: Ent adapter with TransactingRepo, soft-delete, and JSONB containment query.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Direct a.db.TaxCode.* calls without TransactingRepo wrapper fall off the ctx transaction.

key_file_guides.role: Concrete service implementation with transaction.Run, conflict retry in GetOrCreate, and system-managed guard.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Business logic (key derivation, conflict retry) belongs here, not in the adapter.

anti_patterns: Calling adapter methods outside transaction.Run in the service layer — loses atomicity for multi-step operations
Skipping IsManagedBySystem check in new mutating methods — allows external callers to overwrite system-synced records
Implementing business logic (key derivation, conflict retry) inside the adapter instead of the service layer
Adding raw SQL outside sql.P / sql.Selector wrapper in the adapter — bypasses Ent's query builder
Importing app/common in testutils/ — creates import cycles and couples test setup to the full DI graph

decisions.decision: transaction.Run at service layer in addition to entutils.TransactingRepo in adapter
decisions.rationale: Service-level transaction.Run ensures multi-step operations (get-then-update, get-or-create with conflict retry) are atomic; adapter-level TransactingRepo ensures individual DB calls honour the ctx-bound transaction even when called directly.
System sync processes need to update system-managed tax codes without relaxing the public API surface — a typed flag on the input struct keeps the bypass explicit and auditable.

code_examples.scenario: Creating a new tax code through the service
